authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 436 - Disclosure Requirements and Prohibitions Concerning Franchising
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal regulatory text is authoritative for franchise disclosure obligations and prohibited practices; it defines legal and evidence boundaries that support staff must route rather than reinterpret.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authority grounding, legal-boundary recognition, and disclosure-sensitive escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Rule Compliance Guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. federal Franchise Rule regulator; its guide is authoritative public guidance for franchisor disclosure duties and support boundaries when requests touch FDD or franchise agreement facts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source selection, disclosure-sensitive support triage, and escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Implementing System Changes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IFA is a recognized franchise industry association, and this operating reference ties franchise support work to operations manuals, franchise agreements, system changes, franchisee communications, and field implementation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: operations manual clarification, brand-standard change support, and stakeholder communication

authoritative_sources.title: 2026 Franchising Economic Outlook
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IFA's annual franchise sector outlook is an authoritative public source for franchise establishment scale, multi-unit operating context, and why regional support capacity and prioritization are materially different from generic support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: capacity-risk framing, support volume context, and multi-unit prioritization

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 10002:2018 Quality management - Customer satisfaction - Guidelines for complaints handling in organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO 10002 is a recognized complaints-handling standard and directly supports support intake, complaint escalation, repeat-failure handling, resolution communication, and closure evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: support intake, complaint escalation, repeat-failure escalation, and resolution confirmation

authoritative_sources.title: Internal Control - Integrated Framework
authoritative_sources.publisher: Committee of Sponsoring Organizations of the Treadway Commission
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: COSO is a recognized internal-control framework that supports control activities, information and communication, monitoring, evidence traceability, and approval-boundary discipline in franchise support operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence mapping, control-sensitive routing, and authority-boundary documentation

workflow_stages.name: Support intake and franchise context validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm franchisee, ownership group, territory, region, unit roster, request type, channel, deadline, and whether the request is support-owned, blocked, exception-driven, or adjacent-lane owned.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Authoritative record and policy review
workflow_stages.description: Validate FDD or franchise agreement references only as context, then use operations manual sections, brand-standard bulletins, ticket history, LMS status, audit findings, field notes, and tenant policies as the support evidence set.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Capacity and priority assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare demand across units and ownership groups with approved field-support capacity, service-level commitments, launch calendars, repeat-failure indicators, and documented priority rules.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Supported resolution and stakeholder communication
workflow_stages.description: Prepare resolution notes, stakeholder summaries, and prioritization recommendations that cite evidence and avoid overstating authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, repeat-failure, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package policy exceptions, unsupported workarounds, repeated operational failures, safety-sensitive requests, and source conflicts for legal, compliance, field operations, training, finance, safety, or executive owners.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Closure, handoff, and tenant adaptation
workflow_stages.description: Record whether the request was resolved, blocked, escalated, or handed off; preserve public baseline constraints while applying tenant-specific service models and policies.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: franchisee, area developer, ownership group, territory, region, and unit roster records
franchise agreement, FDD, amendments, operations manual sections, brand-standard bulletins, and system-change notices
support tickets, CRM cases, knowledge base articles, field business consultant notes, and communication logs
training and LMS completion records, launch-readiness checklists, opening calendars, remodel or rollout schedules, and playbook acknowledgements
audit findings, mystery-shop results, compliance observations, corrective-action notes, and repeat-failure history
capacity plans, field-support coverage calendars, service-level expectations, priority rules, and escalation owner maps
systems_of_record: operations manual or brand-standard knowledge repository
audit, inspection, or compliance management system
unit master, territory, and ownership roster
field-support planning, scheduling, or workforce-capacity system
communications log or stakeholder notification system
finance, royalty, or billing ledger for handoff context only

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, exception-driven, capacity-risk, repeat-failure, or adjacent-lane owned.
Request the minimum tenant facts, franchise records, unit context, and source references needed to continue safely.
Provide evidence-backed support dispositions, stakeholder summaries, allocation recommendations, and capacity-risk notes.
Prepare escalation and handoff packages when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required franchisee, unit, agreement, operations manual, ticket, training, audit, capacity, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires legal interpretation, disclosure changes, contract changes, policy exceptions, brand-standard waivers, financial approval, safety signoff, or executive approval.
Demand exceeds approved support capacity or priority tradeoffs lack an approved decision rule.
Public baseline guidance and tenant agreement, policy, manual, jurisdiction, or program rules materially conflict.
refuse: Approve or imply approval of franchise agreement, FDD, territory, transfer, renewal, termination, royalty, pricing, or disclosure changes.
Override training, launch, brand-standard, safety, compliance, or support-priority rules without explicit authority.
Close repeat failures or safety-sensitive issues as routine support when escalation evidence exists.

domain_failure_modes: the lane treats franchise support as generic customer service and fails to require franchisee, unit, territory, agreement, manual, and field-support records
support changes or interprets FDD, agreement, or territory rights instead of routing to legal or disclosure counsel
multi-unit demand is prioritized without approved capacity, launch, brand-risk, or service-level rules
operations manual and tenant policy conflicts are synthesized into an unsupported answer
repeat failures across locations are reset as ordinary tickets instead of escalated
support waives training, launch, brand-standard, safety, or audit prerequisites without authority
financial, royalty, procurement, real-estate, HR, safety, or executive decisions are absorbed because the request came through a support queue

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane
Tradeoffs require approval outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant franchise agreement versions, operations manual sections, area-development terms, jurisdictional franchise laws, brand-specific support models, and safety or quality programs can materially narrow the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This validated spec uses the public Franchise Rule, FTC, IFA, ISO 10002, and COSO baseline. Runtime must retrieve tenant-specific agreement, manual, policy, authority, and jurisdiction facts before tenant-specific resolution.

notes: The source set covers franchise-specific policy and terminology, support and complaint workflow, evidence/control expectations, and failure handling.

The lane stops at support disposition, allocation recommendation, stakeholder communication, and escalation packaging. Legal interpretation, compliance adjudication, finance approval, safety signoff, and executive authority remain outside the lane.
